headline: Guy Ritchie's WWII Thriller Detonates on Peacock with A-List Firepower
lede: Alan Ritchson and Henry Cavill's explosive wartime adventure 'The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare' storms streaming platforms this May, promising high-octane historical action.
body:
Guy Ritchie continues his cinematic assault on historical drama with another genre-bending spectacle that transforms wartime narratives. 'The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare' arrives on Peacock, weaponizing star power and Ritchie's signature stylized storytelling.

The film chronicles a covert British special operations unit that pioneered unconventional warfare tactics during World War II. Ritchson leads an ensemble cast that includes Cavill, bringing to life the real-life operatives who executed audacious missions behind enemy lines.

Ritchie's approach blends historical authenticity with kinetic, muscular filmmaking. His track record of reinventing period genres – from 'Sherlock Holmes' to 'The Gentlemen' – suggests this WWII narrative will subvert audience expectations.

Streaming platforms continue their aggressive acquisition of high-concept, star-driven content. Peacock's strategic move to secure this Ritchie production signals ongoing competition for exclusive, must-watch entertainment.
key_facts: Guy Ritchie directs historical action thriller
Alan Ritchson and Henry Cavill star
Peacock secures streaming rights for May release
Based on real WWII special operations unit
